---
title: "Section 5 Case Study"
author: "Qi He"
date: "2026-02-21"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## 1. Data Processing

```{r}
# =========================================================
# Case 1 Pipeline in R (Upgraded carbon story)
# Virginia / PJM / DOM
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 0. Packages
# -----------------------------
required_pkgs <- c(
  "jsonlite", "readr", "dplyr", "tidyr", "ggplot2", "scales", "stringr"
)

installed <- rownames(installed.packages())
for (p in required_pkgs) {
  if (!(p %in% installed)) install.packages(p)
}

library(jsonlite)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(stringr)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Set working directory
# -----------------------------
setwd("D:/Google Drive/R/case1_pipeline_package/case1_pipeline")

getwd()
list.files()

# -----------------------------
# 2. Read parameters and hourly data
# -----------------------------
params <- fromJSON("case1_parameters.json", simplifyVector = FALSE)

hourly <- read_csv("sample_case1_hourly_2025.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(timestamp = as.POSIXct(timestamp, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tz = "UTC"))

# -----------------------------
# 3. Basic checks
# -----------------------------
required_cols <- c(
  "timestamp",
  "lmp_usd_per_mwh",
  "stress_indicator",
  "emissions_tco2_per_mwh",
  "it_load_mw",
  "pue",
  "eta_compute_per_mwh_it",
  "carbon_shadow_price_usd_per_tco2",
  "reliability_valuation_usd_per_compute_unit",
  "location_based_emissions_baseline_tco2_per_mwh"
)

missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(hourly))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop(paste("Missing required columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
}

# -----------------------------
# 4. Extract planning parameters
# -----------------------------
pp <- params$planning_parameters

it_load_mw <- as.numeric(pp$it_load_mw)
pue <- as.numeric(pp$pue)
eta <- as.numeric(pp$eta_compute_per_mwh_it)
lambda <- as.numeric(pp$carbon_shadow_price_usd_per_tco2)
nu <- as.numeric(pp$reliability_valuation_usd_per_compute_unit)
g_lb <- as.numeric(pp$location_based_emissions_baseline_tco2_per_mwh)
```
## 2. Comparative Experiment (structured scenario experiment)

```{r}
# -----------------------------
# 5. Define upgraded option assumptions
# -----------------------------
# We keep the same two stylized designs, but now explicitly allow
# option-specific carbon exposure through carbon_exposure_multiplier.
#
# Interpretation:
# - Connect-and-Manage: full exposure to hourly marginal emissions
# - Associated supply / reduced exposure: lower effective carbon exposure
#
# can tune these values later if needed.

option_assumptions <- tibble::tibble(
  option = c("Connect-and-Manage", "Associated_supply_reduced_exposure"),
  option_label = c("Connect-and-Manage", "Associated supply / reduced exposure"),
  price_multiplier = c(1.00, 1.08),
  carbon_exposure_multiplier = c(1.00, 0.70),
  stress_severity_kappa = c(0.15, 0.03)
)

print(option_assumptions)

# -----------------------------
# 6. Computation function
# -----------------------------
compute_option_components <- function(df, option_name, option_label,
                                      price_multiplier,
                                      carbon_exposure_multiplier,
                                      stress_severity_kappa,
                                      it_load_mw, pue, eta, lambda, nu) {

  df_out <- df %>%
    mutate(
      option = option_name,
      option_label = option_label,
      price_multiplier = price_multiplier,
      carbon_exposure_multiplier = carbon_exposure_multiplier,
      stress_severity_kappa = stress_severity_kappa,

      # Core physical/service mapping
      facility_electricity_mwh = it_load_mw * pue,
      potential_compute = it_load_mw * eta,
      availability_alpha = 1 - stress_severity_kappa * stress_indicator,
      delivered_compute = availability_alpha * potential_compute,

      # Effective decision-facing emissions intensity
      effective_emissions_tco2_per_mwh = carbon_exposure_multiplier * emissions_tco2_per_mwh,

      # Component computations
      electricity_component_usd =
        lmp_usd_per_mwh * price_multiplier * facility_electricity_mwh,

      carbon_component_usd =
        lambda * effective_emissions_tco2_per_mwh * facility_electricity_mwh,

      reliability_component_usd =
        nu * (1 - availability_alpha) * potential_compute,

      crcc_hourly_numerator_usd =
        electricity_component_usd +
        carbon_component_usd +
        reliability_component_usd
    )

  return(df_out)
}

# -----------------------------
# 7. Compute hourly outputs for all options
# -----------------------------
hourly_components_list <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(option_assumptions)), function(i) {
  compute_option_components(
    df = hourly,
    option_name = option_assumptions$option[i],
    option_label = option_assumptions$option_label[i],
    price_multiplier = option_assumptions$price_multiplier[i],
    carbon_exposure_multiplier = option_assumptions$carbon_exposure_multiplier[i],
    stress_severity_kappa = option_assumptions$stress_severity_kappa[i],
    it_load_mw = it_load_mw,
    pue = pue,
    eta = eta,
    lambda = lambda,
    nu = nu
  )
})

hourly_components <- bind_rows(hourly_components_list)

# -----------------------------
# 8. Create summary table
# -----------------------------
option_summary <- hourly_components %>%
  group_by(option, option_label) %>%
  summarise(
    electricity_component_usd_per_delivered_compute =
      sum(electricity_component_usd) / sum(delivered_compute),

    carbon_component_usd_per_delivered_compute =
      sum(carbon_component_usd) / sum(delivered_compute),

    reliability_component_usd_per_delivered_compute =
      sum(reliability_component_usd) / sum(delivered_compute),

    crcc_total_usd_per_delivered_compute =
      sum(crcc_hourly_numerator_usd) / sum(delivered_compute),

    expected_delivered_compute_total = sum(delivered_compute),
    total_potential_compute = sum(potential_compute),
    stress_hours = sum(stress_indicator),
    avg_price_usd_per_mwh = mean(lmp_usd_per_mwh),
    avg_base_emissions_tco2_per_mwh = mean(emissions_tco2_per_mwh),
    avg_effective_emissions_tco2_per_mwh = mean(effective_emissions_tco2_per_mwh),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

print(option_summary)

# -----------------------------
# 9. Export output data
# -----------------------------
write_csv(hourly_components, "case1_option_hourly_components_upgraded.csv")
write_csv(option_summary, "case1_option_summary_upgraded.csv")

```
### 2.1. Figure 5.1

```{r}
# 10. Prepare plotting dataset for Figure 5.1
# -----------------------------
plot_data <- option_summary %>%
  select(
    option_label,
    electricity_component_usd_per_delivered_compute,
    carbon_component_usd_per_delivered_compute,
    reliability_component_usd_per_delivered_compute,
    crcc_total_usd_per_delivered_compute
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      electricity_component_usd_per_delivered_compute,
      carbon_component_usd_per_delivered_compute,
      reliability_component_usd_per_delivered_compute
    ),
    names_to = "component",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    component = recode(
      component,
      "electricity_component_usd_per_delivered_compute" = "Electricity",
      "carbon_component_usd_per_delivered_compute" = "Carbon",
      "reliability_component_usd_per_delivered_compute" = "Reliability"
    )
  )

plot_data$component <- factor(
  plot_data$component,
  levels = c("Electricity", "Carbon", "Reliability")
)

plot_data$option_label <- factor(
  plot_data$option_label,
  levels = c("Connect-and-Manage", "Associated supply / reduced exposure")
)

plot_totals <- option_summary %>%
  mutate(
    option_label = factor(
      option_label,
      levels = c("Connect-and-Manage", "Associated supply / reduced exposure")
    )
  )

# -----------------------------
# 11. Draw Figure 5.1 with total labels
# -----------------------------
fig_5_1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = option_label, y = value, fill = component)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(
    data = plot_totals,
    aes(
      x = option_label,
      y = crcc_total_usd_per_delivered_compute,
      label = round(crcc_total_usd_per_delivered_compute, 1)
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    vjust = -0.45,
    size = 4.2
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.10))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 5.1. Decomposition of CRCC by access/procurement design",
    subtitle = "Virginia / PJM / DOM case, normalized by delivered compute",
    x = NULL,
    y = "USD per delivered compute",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(fig_5_1)

# -----------------------------
# 12. Save figure
# -----------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = "Figure_5_1_CRCC_stacked_bar_upgraded.png",
  plot = fig_5_1,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "Figure_5_1_CRCC_stacked_bar_upgraded.pdf",
  plot = fig_5_1,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5
)
```

### 2.2. Additional Steps
```{r}
# 13. Optional diagnostics
# -----------------------------
# Check total stress hours
hourly %>%
  summarise(total_stress_hours = sum(stress_indicator))

# Check yearly benchmark averages
hourly %>%
  summarise(
    avg_price = mean(lmp_usd_per_mwh),
    avg_emissions = mean(emissions_tco2_per_mwh),
    lb_emissions_baseline = mean(location_based_emissions_baseline_tco2_per_mwh)
  )
```


## 3. Baseline Screening Metric (BSM) vs CRCC
```{r}
# =========================================================
# Figure 5.2 in R
# Baseline Screening Metric (BSM) vs CRCC
# Virginia / PJM / DOM Case
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 0. Packages
# -----------------------------
required_pkgs <- c(
  "jsonlite", "readr", "dplyr", "tidyr", "ggplot2", "scales", "stringr"
)

installed <- rownames(installed.packages())
for (p in required_pkgs) {
  if (!(p %in% installed)) install.packages(p)
}

library(jsonlite)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(stringr)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Set working directory
# -----------------------------
setwd("D:/Google Drive/R/case1_pipeline_package/case1_pipeline")

getwd()
list.files()

# -----------------------------
# 2. Read parameters and hourly data
# -----------------------------
params <- fromJSON("case1_parameters.json", simplifyVector = FALSE)

hourly <- read_csv("sample_case1_hourly_2025.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(timestamp = as.POSIXct(timestamp, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tz = "UTC"))

# -----------------------------
# 3. Basic checks
# -----------------------------
required_cols <- c(
  "timestamp",
  "lmp_usd_per_mwh",
  "stress_indicator",
  "emissions_tco2_per_mwh",
  "it_load_mw",
  "pue",
  "eta_compute_per_mwh_it",
  "carbon_shadow_price_usd_per_tco2",
  "reliability_valuation_usd_per_compute_unit",
  "location_based_emissions_baseline_tco2_per_mwh"
)

missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(hourly))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop(paste("Missing required columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
}

# -----------------------------
# 4. Extract planning parameters
# -----------------------------
pp <- params$planning_parameters

it_load_mw <- as.numeric(pp$it_load_mw)
pue <- as.numeric(pp$pue)
eta <- as.numeric(pp$eta_compute_per_mwh_it)
lambda <- as.numeric(pp$carbon_shadow_price_usd_per_tco2)
nu <- as.numeric(pp$reliability_valuation_usd_per_compute_unit)
g_lb <- as.numeric(pp$location_based_emissions_baseline_tco2_per_mwh)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Define option assumptions
# Same logic as upgraded Figure 5.1
# -----------------------------
option_assumptions <- tibble::tibble(
  option = c("Connect-and-Manage", "Associated_supply_reduced_exposure"),
  option_label = c("Connect-and-Manage", "Associated supply / reduced exposure"),
  price_multiplier = c(1.00, 1.08),
  carbon_exposure_multiplier = c(1.00, 0.70),
  stress_severity_kappa = c(0.15, 0.03)
)

print(option_assumptions)

# -----------------------------
# 6. Common quantities
# -----------------------------
T_hours <- nrow(hourly)
avg_price <- mean(hourly$lmp_usd_per_mwh, na.rm = TRUE)

facility_electricity_mwh_per_hour <- it_load_mw * pue
potential_compute_per_hour <- it_load_mw * eta

sum_E <- T_hours * facility_electricity_mwh_per_hour
sum_Q_pot <- T_hours * potential_compute_per_hour

# -----------------------------
# 7. Function to compute CRCC
# -----------------------------
compute_crcc <- function(df,
                         option_name,
                         option_label,
                         price_multiplier,
                         carbon_exposure_multiplier,
                         stress_severity_kappa,
                         it_load_mw, pue, eta, lambda, nu) {

  tmp <- df %>%
    mutate(
      option = option_name,
      option_label = option_label,
      price_multiplier = price_multiplier,
      carbon_exposure_multiplier = carbon_exposure_multiplier,
      stress_severity_kappa = stress_severity_kappa,

      facility_electricity_mwh = it_load_mw * pue,
      potential_compute = it_load_mw * eta,
      availability_alpha = 1 - stress_severity_kappa * stress_indicator,
      delivered_compute = availability_alpha * potential_compute,

      effective_emissions_tco2_per_mwh = carbon_exposure_multiplier * emissions_tco2_per_mwh,

      electricity_component_usd =
        lmp_usd_per_mwh * price_multiplier * facility_electricity_mwh,

      carbon_component_usd =
        lambda * effective_emissions_tco2_per_mwh * facility_electricity_mwh,

      reliability_component_usd =
        nu * (1 - availability_alpha) * potential_compute,

      crcc_hourly_numerator_usd =
        electricity_component_usd +
        carbon_component_usd +
        reliability_component_usd
    )

  out <- tmp %>%
    summarise(
      option = first(option),
      option_label = first(option_label),
      CRCC =
        sum(crcc_hourly_numerator_usd) / sum(delivered_compute),
      electricity_component_crcc =
        sum(electricity_component_usd) / sum(delivered_compute),
      carbon_component_crcc =
        sum(carbon_component_usd) / sum(delivered_compute),
      reliability_component_crcc =
        sum(reliability_component_usd) / sum(delivered_compute),
      delivered_compute_total = sum(delivered_compute),
      potential_compute_total = sum(potential_compute),
      stress_hours = sum(stress_indicator),
      avg_effective_emissions_tco2_per_mwh = mean(effective_emissions_tco2_per_mwh)
    )

  return(out)
}

# -----------------------------
# 8. Compute CRCC results for all options
# -----------------------------
crcc_results <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(nrow(option_assumptions)), function(i) {
  compute_crcc(
    df = hourly,
    option_name = option_assumptions$option[i],
    option_label = option_assumptions$option_label[i],
    price_multiplier = option_assumptions$price_multiplier[i],
    carbon_exposure_multiplier = option_assumptions$carbon_exposure_multiplier[i],
    stress_severity_kappa = option_assumptions$stress_severity_kappa[i],
    it_load_mw = it_load_mw,
    pue = pue,
    eta = eta,
    lambda = lambda,
    nu = nu
  )
})) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

print(crcc_results)

# -----------------------------
# 9. Compute BSM (Baseline Screening Metric)
# Formula:
# BSM(a,w) = [avg_price * m_(a,w) * sum E + lambda * g_LB * sum E] / sum Q_pot
#
# It intentionally ignores:
# - hourly price variation in the evaluator
# - option-specific decision-facing carbon exposure
# - stress-hour correction in the denominator
# - explicit reliability penalty
# -----------------------------
bsm_results <- option_assumptions %>%
  transmute(
    option,
    option_label,
    BSM =
      (
        avg_price * price_multiplier * sum_E +
          lambda * g_lb * sum_E
      ) / sum_Q_pot,
    avg_price_usd_per_mwh = avg_price,
    location_based_emissions_tco2_per_mwh = g_lb
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

print(bsm_results)

# -----------------------------
# 10. Merge evaluator results
# -----------------------------
comparison_table <- crcc_results %>%
  select(option, option_label, CRCC,
         electricity_component_crcc,
         carbon_component_crcc,
         reliability_component_crcc,
         delivered_compute_total,
         potential_compute_total,
         stress_hours) %>%
  left_join(bsm_results, by = c("option", "option_label")) %>%
  mutate(
    BSM_minus_CRCC = round(BSM - CRCC, 4)
  )

print(comparison_table)

# -----------------------------
# 11. Design-gap calculations
# Reduced exposure minus Connect-and-Manage
# -----------------------------
cm_bsm <- comparison_table %>%
  filter(option == "Connect-and-Manage") %>%
  pull(BSM)

re_bsm <- comparison_table %>%
  filter(option == "Associated_supply_reduced_exposure") %>%
  pull(BSM)

cm_crcc <- comparison_table %>%
  filter(option == "Connect-and-Manage") %>%
  pull(CRCC)

re_crcc <- comparison_table %>%
  filter(option == "Associated_supply_reduced_exposure") %>%
  pull(CRCC)

design_gap <- tibble(
  delta_BSM = round(re_bsm - cm_bsm, 4),
  delta_CRCC = round(re_crcc - cm_crcc, 4),
  ranking_under_BSM = ifelse(re_bsm < cm_bsm,
                             "Reduced exposure preferred",
                             "Connect-and-Manage preferred"),
  ranking_under_CRCC = ifelse(re_crcc < cm_crcc,
                              "Reduced exposure preferred",
                              "Connect-and-Manage preferred")
)

print(design_gap)

# -----------------------------
# 12. Export result tables
# -----------------------------
write_csv(crcc_results, "figure5_2_crcc_results.csv")
write_csv(bsm_results, "figure5_2_bsm_results.csv")
write_csv(comparison_table, "figure5_2_comparison_table.csv")
write_csv(design_gap, "figure5_2_design_gap.csv")

# -----------------------------
# 13. Build plotting dataset
# -----------------------------
plot_data <- comparison_table %>%
  select(option_label, BSM, CRCC) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(BSM, CRCC),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    metric = recode(
      metric,
      "BSM" = "Baseline screening metric",
      "CRCC" = "CRCC"
    ),
    option_label = factor(
      option_label,
      levels = c("Connect-and-Manage", "Associated supply / reduced exposure")
    ),
    metric = factor(
      metric,
      levels = c("Baseline screening metric", "CRCC")
    )
  )

# -----------------------------
# 14. Draw Figure 5.2
# Grouped bar chart with value labels
# -----------------------------
fig_5_2 <- ggplot(plot_data,
                  aes(x = metric, y = value, fill = option_label)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(value, 1)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
    vjust = -0.45,
    size = 4
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.10))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 5.2. Baseline screening metric versus CRCC",
    subtitle = "Decision contrast for the two stylized designs in the Virginia / PJM / DOM case",
    x = NULL,
    y = "USD per compute (lower is better)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(fig_5_2)

# -----------------------------
# 15. Save Figure 5.2
# -----------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = "Figure_5_2_BSM_vs_CRCC.png",
  plot = fig_5_2,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "Figure_5_2_BSM_vs_CRCC.pdf",
  plot = fig_5_2,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5
)

# -----------------------------
# 16. Optional console summary
# -----------------------------
cat("\n==============================\n")
cat("Average price used in BSM:", round(avg_price, 4), "\n")
cat("Location-based emissions factor used in BSM:", round(g_lb, 4), "\n")
cat("==============================\n\n")

print(comparison_table)
print(design_gap)
```
